next_step_text: JUne 24: Shortlisted and we will get informed soon

recent_activities_14d.subject: [Clari - Email Received] RE: Follow‑up on Proposal Submission – RFP 33/2025
[Clari - Email Received] RE: Follow‑up on Proposal Submission – RFP 33/2025

competitive_position.summary: SAP (likely SAP Ariba) is the primary identified competitor and also the incumbent ERP, creating integration and ecosystem lock-in risk. SAP Ariba is the strongest current threat due to incumbent ERP advantage and likely government account presence, though no buyer calls have validated this or revealed other shortlisted vendors. Zycus differentiation hinges on AI-native unified S2P architecture (Agentic AI, single data core, faster deployment, lower TCO) versus SAP's retrofitted AI and fragmented suite. No competitive intelligence from SARS means threat level is inferred from market position and incumbent advantage, not buyer-stated preference. Recency: competitor identified in CRM 24 Mar 2026; no updates since.

competitors.name: SAP (likely SAP Ariba)
competitors.how_we_win: Unified S2P (SAP Ariba is sourcing-only, requires bolt-ons for P2P); Agentic AI (Merlin autonomous negotiation and intake automation vs SAP's chatbot-style Joule); lower TCO via single data core (SAP's modular suite carries hidden integration and licensing costs); proven public-sector success (20+ Australian government entities vs SAP's enterprise focus)

competitors.name: SAP (incumbent ERP)
competitors.quote: Not provided only available info is SAP is ERP
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as the unified S2P layer integrating cleanly to the SAP ERP, not a rip-and-replace, with governed compliance for a revenue agency.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: SAP is the incumbent ERP at SARS, and SAP Ariba is the assumed primary competitor for the S2P RFP given market position and integration advantage.
competitors.source: CRM MEDDPICC Competition field and living memory
competitors.how_we_win: Position Merlin Agentic AI and unified S2P architecture as future-proof versus SAP's bolt-on AI and fragmented modules; emphasize faster deployment, lower TCO, and proven public-sector success (20+ Australian government entities) to counter incumbent inertia.

champion_strength.source: CRM MEDDPICC Champion field, 2026-03-24; zero buyer calls; no contact roles
champion_strength.summary: Champion strength is weak. The CRM MEDDPICC (24 Mar 2026) identifies the CPO (ex-colleague relationship, internal advocate) and the CTO (existing engagement, technical sponsor) as strong potential champions, but neither is named, no contact roles are mapped, and zero buyer calls confirm their advocacy, access to the tender evaluation committee, or current engagement in this RFP cycle. A historical relationship is not a current champion without active participation. The move is to activate the CPO/CTO relationship immediately and secure their guidance on next steps and shortlist intelligence.
champion_strength.champion: CPO (unnamed, ex-colleague) and CTO (unnamed, existing engagement) per CRM MEDDPICC

meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: The CRM MEDDPICC (24 Mar 2026) identifies the Economic Buyer as likely the CPO (described as ex-colleague relationship, owns procurement transformation) and the CTO (existing engagement, owns platform and architecture decision), with secondary influence from the CFO (cost scrutiny, pricing sensitivity). However, none of these individuals are named, no contact roles exist on the opportunity, and zero buyer calls have confirmed their authority, engagement, or sponsorship. The EB is structurally identified by role but not validated by name, active participation, or commercial conversation. This is a hygiene gap (the knowledge exists in CRM role form) but also a partial knowledge gap (no confirmation of who holds these roles at SARS, no evidence of their engagement, and no visibility into whether they are active in the RFP evaluation). The move is to map and engage the named CPO and CTO.

recommended_moves: Activate the ex-colleague CPO relationship to secure shortlist intelligence and next-stage guidance.
Obtain the RFP 33/2025 document, scoring criteria, and post-shortlist evaluation timeline from SARS procurement.
Book a technical deep-dive with the CTO to validate Merlin Agentic AI differentiation versus SAP and other finalists.
